name: mpi4py (MPI for Python — HPC Distributed Computing)

auth.notes: Local library; cluster authentication is at job scheduler level (SLURM/PBS)

performance.notes: Near-native MPI performance via C/Cython bindings; zero-copy with numpy buffers

agent_readiness.notes: MPI-2/3 bindings for Python using Cython. from mpi4py import MPI. comm = MPI.COMM_WORLD. rank = comm.Get_rank(). size = comm.Get_size(). comm.send(data, dest=1); data = comm.recv(source=0). Collective: comm.Bcast(array, root=0). comm.Scatter/Gather. comm.Reduce(sendbuf, recvbuf, op=MPI.SUM). Numpy arrays: comm.Send(np_array, dest=1) (zero-copy). Run: mpirun -n 4 python script.py. Used in HPC scientific computing, parallel ML training, numerical simulations.

gotchas: Requires MPI runtime installed (OpenMPI, MPICH, Intel MPI)
Script must be run via mpirun/mpiexec — cannot test in normal Python interpreter
Deadlocks from send/recv mismatch are common and hard to debug
No dynamic task graph — requires static parallel decomposition
All workers execute same script — SPMD paradigm requires careful rank-based branching
best_when: HPC clusters running SLURM/PBS where MPI is the standard parallelism model
Parallel numerical simulations (fluid dynamics, molecular dynamics, FEM)
MPI-based parallel ML training across many nodes
When interfacing with existing MPI C/Fortran codebases
avoid_when: Cloud-native workloads (use Ray, Dask, or Spark instead)
When Dask distributed covers the use case more easily
summary: mpi4py provides MPI-2/3 bindings for Python HPC computing. MPI.COMM_WORLD.Get_rank(). comm.send/recv for messages. Collective: Bcast, Scatter, Gather, Reduce. Zero-copy numpy transfers with Comm.Send(array). Run via mpirun -n 4 python script.py. BSD-2. Near-native performance via Cython. Used in HPC scientific computing. Requires MPI runtime (OpenMPI/MPICH). For cloud-native parallelism prefer Ray or Dask.
